values: Prototype hardware: ESP32-C3 Super Mini + DRV8833 dual motor driver + two 3 V ERM coin motors
ESP32-C3 Super Mini Wi-Fi + BLE controller board
Generic development-board identifier. Confirm the exact supplier pinout and purchasing MPN before production.
DRV8833 dual H-bridge DC motor driver module
One dual-channel driver controls both motors. Module pin count and nSLEEP pull-up may vary; confirm the selected board.
Place directly across DRV8833 VM and GND. This suggested Murata MPN may be replaced by an equivalent rated part.
Supplier-specific placeholder MPN. Select two identical motors and verify rated current, starting current and mechanical mounting.
1. ESP32-C3 and DRV8833 module identifiers are prototype placeholders; replace them with supplier-orderable MPNs after the exact boards are selected.
2. The 3 V motor supply must support the combined start/stall current of both motors. Never power a motor from an ESP32 GPIO pin.
3. This is a prototype BOM, not a medical-production BOM. Electrical, thermal, EMC, biocompatibility and safety review are required before clinical use.

values: Firmware mapping verified from NeuroVibe.ino: motor 1 uses GPIO 2/3; motor 2 uses GPIO 4/5
Firmware currently drives this low for one-direction operation.
Firmware currently drives this low for one-direction operation.
Connect only if the selected module does not already pull nSLEEP high. Verify module schematic.
Supply must handle both motors' combined start/stall current.
Required common ground between controller and driver.
Place C1 as close as possible to VM and GND. Ceramic capacitor is non-polarized.
Swap the two motor wires only if direction matters mechanically.
Do not connect motor directly to ESP32 pins.
Use the same motor model as M1.
Do not connect motor directly to ESP32 pins.
Use the exact input named by the selected ESP32-C3 Super Mini board. Do not apply 5 V to 3V3.
IMPORTANT: The PWM duty cycle controls average motor voltage/speed; it does not guarantee an exact vibration frequency without calibration or sensor feedback.
